title: Cross-Team Service Level Degradation — 2 Teams Critical Simultaneously
description: Aggregate service level across all four teams dropped to 68% (target: 80%). Auto loans and mortgage servicing are both in critical state simultaneously — first time in 90 days. Root cause: the auto loan spike is pulling cross-trained agents from general banking, which is now understaffed and cascading.

confidence.rationale: Per-team SL is direct telemetry; cascade attribution is causal — Priya's reroute and Okafor's drop align within 6 minutes.

title: Migration Phase 2 Risk — IVR Parity Gap, 12 Days Behind
description: Genesys-to-Dynamics 365 migration Phase 2 (IVR parallel deployment) is 12 days behind schedule. 3 of 14 IVR routing flows have not passed functional parity testing — mortgage payment, fraud escalation, Spanish-language routing. These flows handle 31% of total call volume. If Phase 2 slips past May 15, it compresses Phase 3 (agent migration) and risks the Genesys license expiration window.

confidence.rationale: Schedule slip and 31% volume share are deterministic; downstream Phase 3 compression risk is anchored to 4 prior contact-center migrations.

title: Q2 Overtime Budget Trajectory — Projected 138% by Quarter End
description: Cross-team overtime spend is at 71% of Q2 budget with 10 weeks remaining. Current trajectory projects 138% of budget by quarter end. Top drivers: Priya's team (unplanned marketing-driven spikes) and mortgage servicing (3 experienced agents on extended leave).

confidence.rationale: Spend-to-date is authoritative; quarter-end projection is a linear run-rate that has landed within ±5% on prior quarters.
